titleSomali: Imtixaanka Kama Dambaysta ah ee A2
descriptionSomali: Imtixaankani wuxuu cabbirayaa vocabulary-ga, grammar-ka, reading-ka iyo xirfadaha wada-xiriirka ee heerka A2. Gudub imtixaanka si aad u dhammaystirto heerka A2 ee app-kan oo aad u furto B1.

resources.title: Work and evening study
resources.content:
Sahra works at a small travel office and studies English in the evening. Last September, she joined a six-month course because she wanted to communicate more confidently with international customers. At first, she planned to attend class five evenings a week, but this was difficult after a full day at work. Her teacher helped her create a more practical schedule.

Sahra now attends class on Monday and Wednesday. On Tuesday, she reviews vocabulary for thirty minutes, and at the weekend she writes one short customer message. She has already completed four course projects. Her most recent project was a mock travel plan that included a timetable, hotel information and polite questions for a customer.

Two weeks ago, the office became very busy, and Sahra missed a class. Instead of stopping the course, she asked the teacher for the lesson notes and studied them on Saturday. Her manager has also started giving her simple English tasks at work. Yesterday, Sahra replied to an email about a delayed booking. She checked the dates carefully before sending it.

Sahra still finds telephone conversations challenging because people sometimes speak quickly. Next month, she is going to practise calls with a classmate. If she continues following her weekly plan, she believes she will be ready for a more advanced course later this year.

resources.title: A journey to Lake Town
resources.content:
Visitors can reach fictional Lake Town by bus, train or car. The train is usually the fastest choice, but the station is three kilometres from the town centre. A local bus leaves the station every thirty minutes and stops beside the public library. Travellers who arrive after nine in the evening must use a taxi because the local buses finish early.

The main visitor centre is opposite the library. It provides free maps and information about walking routes. The easiest route follows the lake for two kilometres and ends at Hill Park. The path is wide and suitable for families, although it may become wet after rain. A longer route continues through Green Forest. Visitors should carry water because there are no shops inside the forest.

This Saturday, road repairs will affect buses from the capital. The 8:00 bus will leave at 8:30, and the 10:00 service has been cancelled. Train services will operate normally. The weather forecast says that Saturday morning will be cloudy, with light rain in the afternoon.

People planning a day trip should check their return time before starting a long walk. The final train leaves at 7:15 p.m. Anyone who misses it will have to wait until the next morning. The visitor centre closes at six, but maps are available in a box outside the entrance.

resources.title: A course-booking problem
resources.content:
Abdi registered for a fictional weekend computer course through a community centre’s website. He selected the Saturday morning class and paid forty dollars. A confirmation message arrived immediately, but it showed Sunday afternoon instead. Abdi could attend on Saturday only because he worked on Sundays.

He first checked the form he had saved. It clearly showed Saturday at nine o’clock. He then called the centre, but the information desk was already closed. Abdi sent a short email containing his booking reference, the incorrect day and the time he had originally chosen. He asked the centre to correct the booking or explain how to receive a refund.

The next morning, an employee replied. She apologized and explained that a website error had moved several students to the wrong class. The Saturday class was now full, so she offered Abdi three choices: a Saturday class the following month, an online class starting that week, or a full refund.

Abdi needed the skills for a work project, so waiting a month was not suitable. He chose the online class after checking that its lessons were recorded. The employee changed his booking and sent a new confirmation. Abdi checked the date, price and course link before replying. He thanked her and asked where he could find the course materials. The problem was solved because both messages included clear details and a practical next step.

resources.title: A six-month community goal
resources.content:
Maryan has decided to organize a small reading project in a fictional neighbourhood. She noticed that many children wanted storybooks, but the nearest library was far away. Her goal is to create a weekly reading corner in a room at the community centre.

During the first month, Maryan is going to speak to families and find out which days are convenient. She will also ask local volunteers to help. If at least six volunteers join, the project will open twice a week. Otherwise, it will begin with one Saturday session.

Maryan has already collected thirty-five used books. Some are suitable for young children, while others are better for teenagers. Before the project starts, the volunteers will check every book and organize them by reading level. They might also create simple activity sheets, but this will depend on the time available.

The community centre has offered the room for free for six months. However, the project must keep the room clean and finish before evening classes begin. Maryan plans to record attendance and ask families for feedback after eight weeks. If the project is useful, she will prepare a short report and request support for another six months.

Maryan knows that plans can change. She has therefore chosen a small first step instead of promising a large permanent library. She hopes the project will help children enjoy reading and give neighbours a useful activity to share.

resources.title: Bus timetable
resources.content: Route 8: Central Station 08:10 → Market 08:35 → Airport 09:05. Weekend fare: $6. Return service: 17:40.

resources.title: Train ticket
resources.content: Green Valley Rail • Single • Harbour–Green Valley • 14 August • 10:25 • Coach B, Seat 18 • $24 • Changes before departure only.

resources.title: Shop receipt
resources.content: Mock Home Store • Lamp $18 • Notebook $4 • Subtotal $22 • Discount $2 • Total paid $20 • Card • Return within 14 days with receipt.

resources.title: Hotel booking
resources.content: Lake Hotel • Guest: A. Noor • 2 nights • Check-in 6 June after 2 p.m. • Check-out 8 June before 11 a.m. • Breakfast included • Balance $70.

resources.title: Job advertisement
resources.content: Weekend shop assistant • City Clothes • Saturday and Sunday, 9–5 • Help customers and organize products • Friendly communication required • Apply by 12 May using mock form J-12.

resources.title: Course information
resources.content: Practical English A2 • Tuesdays and Thursdays • 6:30–8:00 p.m. • 8 weeks • Room 4 • Fee $48 • Registration closes 3 September.

resources.title: Weather forecast
resources.content: Friday: 27°C, sunny. Saturday: 23°C, cloudy, rain after 3 p.m. Sunday: 21°C, strong coastal wind. Travel warning applies Sunday morning.

resources.title: Public notice
resources.content: Library lift unavailable 4–5 March. Use the stairs near the main entrance. Ask reception for help accessing books from the first floor.

resources.title: Property advertisement
resources.content: Quiet one-bedroom flat • $260 monthly • Water included • 10 minutes from bus station • No furniture • Available 1 November • Viewing by appointment.

resources.title: Service message form
resources.content: Reference S-204 • Reported: 16 April, 09:20 • Problem: incorrect course date • Status: being checked • Next update: before 5 p.m. on 17 April.

options: I have heard a lot about you.
questions.correctAnswer: I have heard a lot about you.
questions.explanationSomali: “I have heard a lot about you.” ayaa ku habboon context-kan. Function: introduction. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wax badan ayaan kaa maqlay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.

questions.question: Choose the best word for this sentence: The role-play included “___” while introducing people at a fictional training session, so the meaning was clear. (Macnaha loo baahan yahay: fadhiga tababarka)

questions.question: The hotel ___ we booked has free breakfast.
questions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta “that” waxay raacaysaa xeerka relative that.

questions.question: I apologized ___ I had sent the wrong date.
questions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta “because” waxay raacaysaa xeerka reason clause.

questions.question: Arrange correctly: / the station / where / is / Could you tell me / ?
options: Could you tell me where is the station?
Where could you tell me the station is?
Could where the station tell me is?
Could you tell me where the station is?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you tell me where the station is?
questions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta “Could you tell me where the station is?” waxay raacaysaa xeerka indirect question arrangement.

questions.question: Why did Sahra join the English course?
options: To become a travel manager immediately
To communicate better with international customers
To stop working in the evening
questions.correctAnswer: To communicate better with international customers
questions.explanationSomali: Her goal is stated in the first paragraph.

questions.question: How often does Sahra attend class now?
questions.explanationSomali: She attends on Monday and Wednesday.

questions.question: What was Sahra’s most recent project?
questions.explanationSomali: The passage names a mock travel plan.

questions.question: What did Sahra do after missing a class?
options: She requested the notes and studied on Saturday
questions.correctAnswer: She requested the notes and studied on Saturday
questions.explanationSomali: She found a practical way to catch up.

questions.question: Which skill does Sahra still find difficult?
questions.explanationSomali: Fast telephone speech remains challenging.

questions.question: What is the main idea of Sahra’s story?
options: A practical routine helps her combine work and study
questions.correctAnswer: A practical routine helps her combine work and study
questions.explanationSomali: The passage focuses on a sustainable learning plan.

questions.question: Why might a late visitor need a taxi in Lake Town?
options: The train station closes at six
questions.explanationSomali: The local bus service finishes early.

questions.question: Where is the visitor centre?
questions.explanationSomali: The location is given directly.

questions.question: What should walkers carry in Green Forest?
questions.explanationSomali: There are no shops inside the forest.

questions.question: What has happened to the 10:00 Saturday bus?
questions.explanationSomali: The service update states this clearly.

questions.question: What is the best title for this passage?
options: Planning a visit to Lake Town
questions.correctAnswer: Planning a visit to Lake Town
questions.explanationSomali: The passage provides travel and visitor information.

questions.question: What was wrong with Abdi’s first confirmation?
options: It showed Sunday instead of Saturday
questions.correctAnswer: It showed Sunday instead of Saturday
questions.explanationSomali: The day did not match his saved form.

questions.question: Why could Abdi not wait for next month’s class?
options: He needed the skills for a work project
questions.correctAnswer: He needed the skills for a work project
questions.explanationSomali: His work need made the delay unsuitable.

questions.question: What did Abdi check in the new confirmation?
options: The date, price and course link
questions.correctAnswer: The date, price and course link
questions.explanationSomali: He verified three important details.

questions.question: What does “full” mean in “the Saturday class was full”?
questions.explanationSomali: In this context, full means all places were taken.

questions.question: Why was the problem solved successfully?
options: Both sides shared clear details and a next step
questions.correctAnswer: Both sides shared clear details and a next step
questions.explanationSomali: Clear communication supported the solution.

questions.question: Why does Maryan want to start the project?
options: The nearest library is far from local children
questions.correctAnswer: The nearest library is far from local children
questions.explanationSomali: The need is explained at the beginning.

questions.question: What will happen if six volunteers join?
options: The reading corner will open twice a week
questions.correctAnswer: The reading corner will open twice a week
questions.explanationSomali: This is the passage’s first conditional plan.

questions.question: How many books has Maryan collected?
questions.explanationSomali: The exact number appears in paragraph three.

questions.question: Why must sessions finish before evening classes?
options: The same room is used for those classes
questions.correctAnswer: The same room is used for those classes
questions.explanationSomali: The centre has conditions for using its room.

questions.question: When will Maryan ask families for feedback?
questions.explanationSomali: The evaluation time is stated directly.

questions.question: What will Maryan do if the project is useful?
options: Prepare a report and request more support
questions.correctAnswer: Prepare a report and request more support
questions.explanationSomali: Her next step depends on the result.

questions.question: What is the writer’s purpose?
options: To advertise an official library job
To describe a realistic community action plan
questions.correctAnswer: To describe a realistic community action plan
questions.explanationSomali: The text explains goals, steps and conditions.

questions.question: True or false: Maryan has promised a permanent library.
options: The passage does not mention a project
questions.explanationSomali: She deliberately begins with a small temporary project.

questions.question: Which quality best describes Maryan’s plan?
questions.explanationSomali: The plan has conditions, stages and feedback.

questions.question: What time does Route 8 reach the airport?
questions.explanationSomali: The final outward time is 09:05.

questions.question: How long is the journey from Central Station to the airport?
questions.explanationSomali: 09:05 minus 08:10 equals 55 minutes.

questions.question: Which seat is shown on the train ticket?
questions.explanationSomali: The ticket gives both coach and seat.

questions.question: How much money did the shop discount?
questions.explanationSomali: The subtotal was $22 and total was $20.

questions.question: What must the customer keep for a return?
questions.explanationSomali: The return condition requires the receipt.

questions.question: When must the hotel guest leave?
questions.explanationSomali: This is the stated check-out time.

questions.question: What is included in the hotel booking?
questions.explanationSomali: Breakfast included is printed in the confirmation.

questions.question: What is one main duty in the job advertisement?
questions.explanationSomali: The advertisement lists customer help.

questions.question: How many classroom hours does the course provide each week?
questions.explanationSomali: Two 90-minute classes equal three hours.

questions.question: Which day has a travel warning?
questions.explanationSomali: The warning applies Sunday morning.

questions.question: When is Saturday rain expected?
questions.explanationSomali: The forecast gives the time directly.

questions.question: What should someone do if stairs are difficult?
questions.explanationSomali: The notice gives an accessible help action.

questions.question: Which cost is included in the flat’s monthly price?
questions.explanationSomali: The advertisement says water included.

questions.question: How can a person view the flat?
questions.explanationSomali: Viewing is available by appointment.

questions.question: What is the current status of reference S-204?
questions.explanationSomali: The form states “being checked”.

questions.question: You meet a new colleague. What is the best opening?
options: Hello, I’m Hani. It’s nice to meet you.
questions.correctAnswer: Hello, I’m Hani. It’s nice to meet you.
questions.explanationSomali: This is a friendly introduction.

questions.question: A colleague asks, “Could you send me the schedule?”
options: Of course. I’ll send it after lunch.
questions.correctAnswer: Of course. I’ll send it after lunch.
questions.explanationSomali: It accepts the request and gives a clear time.

questions.question: You want permission to use a desk.
options: May I use this desk for an hour?
questions.correctAnswer: May I use this desk for an hour?
questions.explanationSomali: May I is a polite permission form.

questions.question: A visitor is carrying two heavy boxes.
options: Would you like me to help you?
questions.correctAnswer: Would you like me to help you?
questions.explanationSomali: This is an appropriate offer.

questions.question: You arrived late for an appointment.
options: I’m sorry I’m late. The bus was delayed.
questions.correctAnswer: I’m sorry I’m late. The bus was delayed.
questions.explanationSomali: It apologizes and briefly explains.

questions.question: Someone helped you complete a form.
options: Thank you for explaining the form.
questions.correctAnswer: Thank you for explaining the form.
questions.explanationSomali: It directly thanks the helper.

questions.question: A friend invites you, but you cannot attend.
options: You have to attend for me.
Thanks for inviting me, but I’m not available then.
questions.correctAnswer: Thanks for inviting me, but I’m not available then.
questions.explanationSomali: It declines politely and gives context.

questions.question: Your friend says, “I have a bad headache.”
options: You should rest and speak to a health professional if it continues.
questions.correctAnswer: You should rest and speak to a health professional if it continues.
questions.explanationSomali: It gives safe general advice.

questions.question: A teammate suggests meeting at three, and you agree.
questions.explanationSomali: This is a natural agreement.

questions.question: You disagree with a plan but want to be polite.
options: I see your point, but Friday may be more convenient.
questions.correctAnswer: I see your point, but Friday may be more convenient.
questions.explanationSomali: It acknowledges the other view before disagreeing.

questions.question: You did not understand an instruction.
options: Could you explain the last step again, please?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you explain the last step again, please?
questions.explanationSomali: It asks for clarification politely.

questions.question: A shop sent the wrong product.
options: I ordered the blue model, but I received the black one.
Your shop is the worst place.
I am receiving it next year.
questions.correctAnswer: I ordered the blue model, but I received the black one.
questions.explanationSomali: It states the complaint clearly and factually.

questions.question: You answer a workplace telephone call.
options: Good morning, City Office. How can I help?
questions.correctAnswer: Good morning, City Office. How can I help?
questions.explanationSomali: It is a professional phone greeting.

questions.question: A customer says an order is late.
options: I’m sorry about the delay. Let me check the reference.
questions.correctAnswer: I’m sorry about the delay. Let me check the reference.
questions.explanationSomali: It apologizes and begins solving the problem.

questions.question: You need a caller to speak more slowly.
options: Could you speak a little more slowly, please?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you speak a little more slowly, please?
questions.explanationSomali: This is a clear polite request.

questions.question: Someone says, “Thank you for your help.”
questions.explanationSomali: You’re welcome responds to thanks.

questions.question: You want to arrange another appointment.
options: Could we move the appointment to Thursday afternoon?
questions.correctAnswer: Could we move the appointment to Thursday afternoon?
questions.explanationSomali: It makes a specific polite request.

questions.question: A friend says, “I passed my course.”
questions.explanationSomali: Congratulations fits good news.

questions.question: You need more information about a job.
options: Could you tell me what the working hours are?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you tell me what the working hours are?
questions.explanationSomali: It is a polite indirect question.

questions.question: A customer wants a refund, but you need the receipt.
options: Certainly. Could I see your receipt first?
questions.correctAnswer: Certainly. Could I see your receipt first?
questions.explanationSomali: It responds politely and explains the next action.

questions.question: A colleague offers help, but you have finished.
options: Thank you, but I’ve already completed the task.
questions.correctAnswer: Thank you, but I’ve already completed the task.
questions.explanationSomali: It declines the offer politely.

questions.question: You accidentally sent the wrong date.
options: I apologize for the mistake. I’ll send the correct date now.
I am agree with the error.
questions.correctAnswer: I apologize for the mistake. I’ll send the correct date now.
questions.explanationSomali: It accepts responsibility and gives a solution.

questions.question: You want to confirm what you heard.
options: Did you say the meeting starts at nine?
questions.correctAnswer: Did you say the meeting starts at nine?
questions.explanationSomali: It checks one specific detail.

questions.question: A hotel room is too noisy.
options: Could I move to a quieter room, please?
questions.correctAnswer: Could I move to a quieter room, please?
questions.explanationSomali: It requests a practical solution politely.

questions.question: A caller asks for a manager who is busy.
options: She is in a meeting. Would you like to leave a message?
questions.correctAnswer: She is in a meeting. Would you like to leave a message?
questions.explanationSomali: It provides information and offers a next step.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “The employee must to arrive early.”
options: The employee to must arrive early.
questions.explanationSomali: Must waxaa raaca base verb aan “to” lahayn.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “We have booked the tickets last Friday.”
options: We have book the tickets last Friday.
We booked the tickets last Friday.
We did booked the tickets last Friday.
questions.correctAnswer: We booked the tickets last Friday.
questions.explanationSomali: Finished time “last Friday” wuxuu qaataa Past Simple.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “Hodan enjoys to watch documentaries.”
questions.explanationSomali: Enjoy waxaa raaca verb-ing.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “There have a pharmacy near the bank.”
options: There is a pharmacy near the bank.
There are a pharmacy near the bank.
It have a pharmacy near the bank.
There is pharmacy are near the bank.
questions.correctAnswer: There is a pharmacy near the bank.
questions.explanationSomali: Hal meel/shay waxaa loo adeegsadaa there is.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “Do you know where does the bus stop?”
options: Do you know where stops the bus?
Do you know where the bus stops?
Do you know where does stop the bus?
Do you know the bus where does stop?
questions.correctAnswer: Do you know where the bus stops?
questions.explanationSomali: Indirect question-ku wuxuu leeyahay statement word order.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “I am agree with your suggestion.”
options: I do agree withing your suggestion.
questions.explanationSomali: Agree waa verb; “am” looma baahna.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “The driver didn’t checked the address.”
options: The driver didn’t checks the address.
The driver not checked the address.
The driver doesn’t checked the address.
The driver didn’t check the address.
questions.correctAnswer: The driver didn’t check the address.
questions.explanationSomali: Didn’t waxaa raaca base verb.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “She gave me many useful advices.”
options: She gave me a lot of useful advice.
She gave me an useful advice.
She gave me many useful advice pieceses.
questions.correctAnswer: She gave me a lot of useful advice.
questions.explanationSomali: Advice waa uncountable.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “The students were boring during the film.”
options: The students were bore during the film.
The students were bored during the film.
The students bored the film feeling.
The students were boredom during film.
questions.correctAnswer: The students were bored during the film.
questions.explanationSomali: Bored wuxuu sheegayaa sida dadka dareemeen.

questions.question: Choose the correct version: “If the weather will improve, we will walk.”
options: If the weather improved, we will walked.
If will the weather improve, we walk.
If the weather improves, we will walk.
If the weather improving, we will walking.
questions.correctAnswer: If the weather improves, we will walk.
questions.explanationSomali: First conditional if-clause-ku wuxuu qaataa Present Simple.

questions.question: Dooro tarjumaadda saxda ah: “Waxaan booqday xarunta shalay.”
options: I have visited the centre yesterday.
I visit the centre now yesterday.
I am visit the centre yesterday.
questions.explanationSomali: Yesterday wuxuu u baahan yahay Past Simple.

questions.question: “Ma ii sheegi kartaa goorta tareenku baxayo?”
options: Could you tell me when does the train leave?
Tell me when leaves the train could?
Could you tell me when the train leaves?
Could when the train leaves tell me?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you tell me when the train leaves?
questions.explanationSomali: Indirect question-ku wuxuu isticmaalaa statement order.

questions.question: “Waxay qorshaynaysaa inay bilowdo koorso cusub.”
options: She going start a new course.
She is going to started a new course.
She will to starting a new course.
She is going to start a new course.
questions.correctAnswer: She is going to start a new course.
questions.explanationSomali: Going to waxaa raaca base verb.

questions.question: “Haddii roob da’o, guriga ayaan joogi doonaa.”
options: If it rains, I will stay at home.
If it will rain, I stay at home.
If it rained, I will stayed home.
If rains, I am stay home.
questions.correctAnswer: If it rains, I will stay at home.
questions.explanationSomali: First conditional: present + will.

questions.question: “Weli ma helin xaqiijinta.”
options: I didn’t received the confirmation yet.
I haven’t received the confirmation yet.
I am not receiving yet yesterday.
questions.correctAnswer: I haven’t received the confirmation yet.
questions.explanationSomali: Yet waxaa si dabiici ah ula socda Present Perfect.

questions.question: “Fadlan ma hubin kartaa lambarka tixraaca?”
options: Could you check the reference number, please?
Could you checking the reference number?
questions.correctAnswer: Could you check the reference number, please?
questions.explanationSomali: Could + base verb waa codsi edeb leh.

questions.question: “Qolkani kuma filna kulanka.”
options: This room is too enough for meeting.
This room has not many large.
This room is not enough large meeting.
This room is not large enough for the meeting.
questions.correctAnswer: This room is not large enough for the meeting.
questions.explanationSomali: Adjective + enough ayaa la isticmaalaa.

questions.question: “Waxay halkan ka shaqaynaysay tan iyo 2023.”
questions.explanationSomali: Since wuxuu tilmaamaa starting point.

questions.question: Dooro macnaha guud: “The appointment has been moved to Friday afternoon.”
options: Ballanta waxaa loo beddelay Jimcaha galabtii.
questions.correctAnswer: Ballanta waxaa loo beddelay Jimcaha galabtii.
questions.explanationSomali: Moved here means the scheduled time changed.

questions.question: “You don’t have to bring a laptop; the centre provides one.”
options: Laptop lama oggola in xarunta la geeyo.
Waa qasab inaad laba laptop keento.
Laptop inaad keento khasab ma aha; xarunta ayaa mid bixisa.
Xaruntu ma laha wax kombiyuutar ah.
questions.correctAnswer: Laptop inaad keento khasab ma aha; xarunta ayaa mid bixisa.
questions.explanationSomali: Don’t have to waxay ka dhigan tahay khasab ma aha.

questions.question: “Although the flat is small, it has enough storage.”
options: Gurigu wuu weyn yahay, laakiin kayd ma leh.
Sababta gurigu u yar yahay waa kaydka.
Gurigu aad buu ugu yar yahay in la arko.
Inkastoo gurigu yar yahay, wuxuu leeyahay meel kayd oo ku filan.
questions.correctAnswer: Inkastoo gurigu yar yahay, wuxuu leeyahay meel kayd oo ku filan.
questions.explanationSomali: Although wuxuu muujinayaa contrast.

questions.question: “Please keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.”
options: Fadlan hayso rasiidka haddii aad u baahato inaad shayga celiso.
Rasiidka tuur markaad dukaanka ka baxdo.
Shayga waa in hadda la celiyaa.
questions.correctAnswer: Fadlan hayso rasiidka haddii aad u baahato inaad shayga celiso.
questions.explanationSomali: The message advises keeping proof of purchase.

questions.question: “The train might be delayed because of strong winds.”
options: Tareenka waxaa laga yaabaa inuu dabaylo xooggan dartood dib u dhaco.
Dabayshu waxay joojisay dhammaan safarrada weligeed.
questions.correctAnswer: Tareenka waxaa laga yaabaa inuu dabaylo xooggan dartood dib u dhaco.
questions.explanationSomali: Might expresses possibility, not certainty.

questions.question: “I’m following up on the message I sent on Monday.”
options: Waxaan fariinta diri doonaa Isniinta dambe.
Waxaan daba socdaa fariintii aan diray Isniintii.
Fariintu waxay timid ka hor shaqada.
questions.correctAnswer: Waxaan daba socdaa fariintii aan diray Isniintii.
questions.explanationSomali: Following up means requesting an update.

questions.question: “Would you mind speaking a little more slowly?”
options: Waa inaad si degdeg ah u baxdaa.
questions.explanationSomali: This is a polite request to reduce speaking speed.
